customManagers.description: Update python-version in GitHub Actions workflow files

packageRules.description: Soak major updates 30 days before raising a PR — the most breakage-prone, so give them the longest bake. CVE remediation is unaffected (vulnerabilityAlerts ignores the soak).

requirements.txt and dev-requirements.txt are pip-compile lockfiles generated from setup.py (install_requires, and install_requires + the dev extra respectively). The pip-compile manager owns them and applies bumps to the constraints in setup.py, then regenerates the lockfiles. Disable the default pip_requirements manager on the lockfiles and the pip_setup manager on setup.py so they don't emit competing, drifting PRs against the compile source.

The python-version regex manager tracks a bare X.Y.Z version with no digest slot, so digest pinning (enabled repo-wide by config:best-practices) would fail with 'Digest is not updated'. Disable it for regex-managed docker deps.
Group all Python version updates together so the CI/CD workflows bump in a single PR
